news.summary: During an earnings call, Walmart CEO Doug McMillon disclosed plans to raise prices on certain items due to US tariffs. Despite a recent tariff rate cut, McMillon stated that the tariffs' impact was substantial, leading to increased costs that would now be transferred to consumers. The retailer aims to maintain competitive pricing but finds it challenging to absorb the tariffs, even following the recent reductions. This decision comes following the ongoing trade tensions. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Walmart will raise prices due to Trump tariffs, CEO says. Despite rate reductions, costs too high. Others may follow suit.

news.headline: TAT Techs (NASDAQ: TATT) to report EPS of $0.31 on May 19, 2025. Investors eagerly await news on surpassing estimates.

news.summary: In Boulder, CO, on May 16, 2025, FST Corp, (Nasdaq: KBSX), a prominent producer of steel and graphite golf shafts and related services, revealed that for the fiscal year closing on December 31, 2024, the Company generated $36,499,644 in revenue. This marked a noteworthy growth of 27% compared to the previous fiscal year, which saw revenue at $28,730,549 during the period ending on December 31, 2023.
news.headline: FST Corp. reports 27% revenue growth in 2024, reaching $36.5M vs. $28.7M in 2023. Leader in golf shafts.

news.summary: KIOXIA and Linus Media Group, based in San Jose, California, have reached an impressive milestone, setting a new record.
news.headline: KIOXIA and Linus Media Group set world record for Pi calculation in SAN JOSE, California.

news.summary: Bitwise's chief investment officer, Matt Hougan, predicts Bitcoin will reach $200,000 by the end of 2025 due to heightened institutional demand. He cites figures like miners producing 165,000 BTC this year, publicly traded companies buying more, and ETF inflows reaching $6 billion. Bitwise has nearly $4 billion in assets under management through its Bitwise Bitcoin ETF as of May 14. Hougan believes the market's liquidity from institutional demand may render the four-year halving cycle a thing of the past. One major player, Strategy, holds 568,840 BTC in its treasury, impacting Bitcoin's supply and causing it to be deflationary. Analysts have varied predictions, with some forecasting a $1 million Bitcoin price tag in the coming decade, while others like Arthur Hayes expect it to hit $1 million in three years.
news.headline: Bitcoin's $200K 2025 target affirmed due to supply crunch & rising institutional demand per Bitwise CIO.ETF inflows at $6B.474,000 BTC bought by firms; Strategy leads reserve strategy with 568,840 BTC. Bitcoin supply deflationary; -2.33% annual rate. Predictions of $1M BTC in 3 yrs; rising macroeconomic uncertainties fueled by central bank liquidity injections.

news.summary: On Friday, the stock market displayed resilience as it made modest gains and maintained recent momentum. EBay emerged as a top performer. The Dow Jones index sustained its strength following a significant uptrend, with attention also on Warren Buffett's investment moves. The market's performance was covered live on Investor's Business Daily, capturing the upbeat tone with the Dow Jones holding firm after a bullish increase.
news.headline: Stock market holds strong after bullish gain, Dow Jones edges higher on Friday. eBay leads new entry. Buffett moves in focus.

news.summary: The XRP cryptocurrency has experienced a 1% decline after a $50 million settlement proposal between Ripple Labs and the U.S. SEC was rejected by a U.S. District Judge. Despite this legal development, some crypto analysts are spotting indications of a potential bullish reversal. As of now, the cryptocurrency figures stand at $2.42 for XRP with a market cap of $4.9 billion, showing a 24-hour trend of -1.3% and a 7-day trend of +3.8%. In comparison, Bitcoin (BTC) is priced at $104,362.42 with a market cap of $2.07 trillion, indicating a 1.2% increase in the last 24 hours and a 1.5% increase over the past 7 days. Meanwhile, Ethereum (ETH) is priced at $2,590.27 with a market cap of $312.8 billion, showing a 1.2% increase in the last 24 hours and a significant 12.5% increase over the past 7 days. Notable trader Dark Defender has suggested that XRP has completed its final correction wave on the 4-hour chart and foresees a potential move towards $2.55, setting the ultimate short-term target at $3.333. Key support levels on the chart have been identified as well. For more information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: XRP drops 1% as judge rejects $50M settlement with SEC, price at $2.42 with market cap $4.9B. Analysts predict bullish reversal.

news.summary: After striking a deal to acquire Cox Communications, Charter Communications, the parent company of Spectrum, is set to pay a total of $21.9 billion, including both equity and debt. Following the announcement, Charter's stock surged, surpassing a significant milestone. This acquisition marks a significant move for Charter Communications, positioning them for potential growth and expansion in the telecommunications industry.
news.headline: Charter's stock rises after $21.9 billion deal for Cox Communications.

news.summary: On Friday, Wall Street saw its fifth day of gains, with major indexes slightly higher in New York. The S&P 500 rose 0.1% to 5,925, reaching its highest level since early March, while the Nasdaq 100 remained steady after a strong previous session. Investors were processing recent trade breakthroughs, leading to cautious optimism amid macro concerns. Healthcare stocks saw a rebound, supported by a rally in Eli Lilly & Co. (NYSE: LLY), while the energy sector lagged due to cooling crude prices. The U.S. dollar strengthened following a bleak consumer sentiment report from the University of Michigan, indicating stagflationary signals not seen in recent decades. This trend and more can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Stocks rise for fifth day, S&P 500 up 0.1% to 5,925, Nasdaq 100 steady. Dollar rebounds on stagflation fears.

news.summary: In its latest report, the National Health Organization stated that the recent surge in COVID-19 cases has reached alarming levels, with a record-breaking 10,000 new cases reported in the past 24 hours. This brings the total number of confirmed cases in the country to over 250,000. The organization also reported a concerning increase in hospitalizations, with over 2,500 patients currently receiving treatment for the virus. In response to the escalating crisis, the government has announced stricter lockdown measures in hotspot areas to curb the spread of the virus. The public is urged to adhere to safety protocols and get vaccinated to help mitigate the impact of the pandemic.
news.headline: Coinbase stock surges after joining S&P 500; concerns over hack and probe fade.

news.summary: The CEO of Digital Chamber stated that despite the stalling of stablecoin legislation in the US Senate, the bill is expected to pass soon to protect the US dollar's global presence. The legislation, known as the GENIUS Act of 2025, is crucial, and failing to pass it before the midterm elections in 2026 could lead to negative impacts on the crypto markets. The bill faced obstacles in a recent Senate vote due to Democratic concerns over Donald Trump's involvement in crypto, but the latest version removing references to Trump's family is anticipated to pass by the end of May. Industry insiders are optimistic about the bill's eventual approval as negotiations continue, emphasizing the importance of bipartisan support for stablecoin regulations in upholding US monetary dominance internationally.
news.headline: US Senate to pass Stablecoin bill soon after minor setback, aiming to protect US dollar dominance in global markets. Bill called GENIUS Act of 2025. Trump's crypto ties blamed for bill's initial failure. New version expected to pass Senate by end of May. Magazine highlights shift of crypto from banking disruptor to stablecoin player.

news.summary: A recent report on the Nigeria Data Center Market provided by ResearchAndMarkets.com indicates that in 2024, the market was valued at USD 278 million. The report forecasts a significant growth trajectory, with projections indicating that the market is set to reach USD 671 million by 2030. This equates to a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 15.82%. This analysis thoroughly examines the market share within Nigeria, detailing both current operational data centers as well as planned investments in IT, electrical, and mechanical infrastructure.
news.headline: Nigeria Data Center Market to reach $671M by 2030 - from $278M in 2024. Growth opportunities in IT, electrical, mechanical infrastructure.

news.summary: On May 16, 2025, Eric M. Hansen, the Senior Vice President of Preferred Employers Insurance, a Berkley Company, has been re-elected as the Chair of the California Workers' Compensation Institute (CWCI) Board of Directors for the year 2025. The CWCI focuses on improving research and policies related to workers' compensation. This marks Hansen's continued leadership in the organization, showcasing his ongoing commitment to the industry.
news.headline: Eric Hansen of Preferred Employers Insurance re-elected as 2025 CWCI Chair. Leading California Workers' Compensation Institute Board.

news.summary: An announcement was made in Eagle, Idaho on May 16, 2025, by The Pennant Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: PNTG), which oversees a range of affiliated home health, hospice, and senior living companies, revealing the addition of Suzanne D. Snapper to its board of directors. This decision follows her successful election by shareholders during the Company's annual shareholder gathering.
news.headline: The Pennant Group, Inc. appoints Suzanne Snapper to board of directors after shareholder vote at annual meeting.

news.summary: In Dublin, a report on the U.S. Data Center Construction Market has been recently included by ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market was worth USD 48.18 billion in 2024 and is estimated to hit USD 112.33 billion by 2030, showing a growth rate of 15.15% during the forecast period. The U.S. data center construction sector accommodates key global colocation operators like Equinix, Digital Realty, QTS Realty Trust, CyrusOne, and Aligned Data Centers.
news.headline: US Data Center Construction Market valued at $48.18B in 2024 projected to reach $112.33B by 2030 with a CAGR of 15.15%. Major investors include Apple, AWS, Microsoft, Google, and Meta.

news.summary: Flowers Foods, Inc. (NYSE: FLO) released its first-quarter financial results, reporting an adjusted earnings per share of 35 cents, falling short of the analyst consensus of 38 cents. The company saw quarterly sales of $1.55 billion, a 1.4% decrease from the previous year and below the expected $1.60 billion. Adjusted EBITDA rose by 1.6% to $162.0 million, accounting for 10.4% of net sales and marking a 30-basis point increase. Following the acquisition of Simple Mills, Flowers Foods noted that it added $24.3 million in net sales, incurred a net loss of $4.2 million, and contributed $3.6 million to adjusted EBITDA. Additionally, the FDA is addressing synthetic food dyes in a significant nutrition reform initiative. Despite challenges such as economic uncertainty and higher category declines than anticipated in the first quarter, Flowers Foods emphasized the strength of its primary brands.
news.headline: Flowers Foods reported Q1 EPS of 35 cents, missing estimates. Quarterly sales were $1.55B (1.4% decrease), with adjusted EBITDA of $162M. Simple Mills acquisition contributed $24.3M in net sales, with a net loss of $4.2M. Company cuts annual outlook due to tariffs impact.

news.summary: In Palo Alto, California on May 16, 2025, Vectara, a prominent company specializing in Enterprise Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and AI-driven Agents & Assistants, revealed that it has secured a contract with Anywhere Integrated Services (AIS), a subsidiary of Anywhere Real Estate Inc. This collaboration marks a significant milestone for Vectara and showcases its expertise in the industry.

news.summary: Flagstaff, Arizona, May 16, 2025 - An innovative concept known as the Awning and Rooftop Tent (ARTT) is set to revolutionize outdoor adventures with its premium, lightweight, and low-profile design. This all-in-one awning and rooftop tent system is specifically designed to seamlessly integrate with the Jeep® Wrangler. Interested individuals can witness the unveiling of this new system at the upcoming Overland Expo West.
news.headline: Jeep® unveils ARTT concept at Overland Expo West: premium rooftop tent system for Jeep® Wrangler.

news.summary: Small restaurant owners are invited to apply for the Backing Historic Small Restaurants (BHSR) grant program, facilitated by the National Trust for Historic Preservation and backed by American Express. The initiative aims to assist small and independent restaurants in preserving historic establishments. Applications for the program are currently being accepted.
news.headline: National Trust & American Express start 5th year of BHSR grant program for small restaurants. Apply now.

news.summary: On Friday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a decline following unexpected inflation data. Applied Materials, a supplier for Nvidia, saw a significant drop in its stock value after releasing its earnings report. The unexpected inflation numbers impacted the market, leading to a noticeable decrease in the Dow Jones. This news was reported by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Dow Jones falls due to surprise inflation data; Nvidia supplier dives on earnings. Applied Materials plunges.

news.summary: On Friday, the stock market maintained significant increases, with the Nasdaq composite seeing a weekly surge of over 6%. Tesla stock displayed strong performance in the early hours of trading. The Dow Jones showed some fluctuations, while attention was on Warren Buffett's moves. Live coverage of these developments was provided on Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Stock market sees strong gains, Nasdaq up 6% for the week; Tesla stock performs well. Dow Jones fluctuates. Warren Buffett moves discussed.

news.summary: BioMarin announced it has agreed to acquire Inozyme Pharma for a total of $270 million. The acquisition caused Inozyme Pharma's shares to experience a significant increase by triple digits. This takeover by BioMarin resulted in a substantial gain for Inozyme Pharma's stock value.
news.headline: BioMarin's $270M acquisition of Inozyme Pharma leads to triple-digit gain in shares.
